reasoning: Output B provides a precise, bounded execution plan: read sprint-status.yaml from the known path, summarize status, report to user, and explicitly stop without entering the main loop or spawning sub-agents. It clearly states what the skill does NOT do for a status query. Output A takes a broader search-based approach (searching for status files by pattern rather than going to the known path), acknowledges it might not know the exact path, and passes the 'no sub-agents' assertion only by default (no architecture to invoke) rather than by design. Output B demonstrates deliberate scope control; Output A describes a reasonable but less precise approach.

strengths: Would eventually find and read sprint-status.yaml
Would not spawn sub-agents (no architecture to do so)
Good comparison table at the end
weaknesses: Uses broad file search instead of known path
Would not invoke bmad-help if file is missing
Passes 'no sub-agents' assertion by default, not by design
Might over-act if user phrasing were slightly different

strengths: Goes directly to known sprint-status.yaml path
Explicitly handles both file-exists and file-missing cases
Invokes bmad-help if file is missing
Explicitly states 'Do NOT continue the main loop'
Lists what the skill does NOT do (clear scope boundary)
